# Improving COMP110: Analyzing Prior Experience
## By: Grace Kennedy

In this analysis, I explored whether students with little or no prior coding experience reported lower understanding, higher difficulty, and lower confidence in COMP110. This would help evaluate whether beginner-level modules could be useful for supporting students without programming experience. I looked at the difficulty, prior_exp, and understanding data.

<!-- This is a comment. Below, you'll see code for inserting an image. To make this image appear, update <custom-path>. To add an image, save it inside the imgs folder of this repository. -->
<img src="/ex09/chart1.png" alt="How many students are beginners" width="500"/>
<img src="/ex09/chart2.png" alt="Prior experience vs difficulty" width="500"/>
<img src="/ex09/chart3.png" alt="Prior experience vs understanding" width="500"/>
<img src="/ex09/chart4.png" alt="Overall understanding levels" width="500"/>

Based on my graphs, students with little or no prior coding experience report a lower understanding and a higher difficulty in this class. This suggests that beginners may face a disadvantage upon entering the course, which may lead to lower confidence while learning in this class. This could mean that implementing beginner modules might benefit these students, providing them with a good amount of exposure to build confidence and close the gap between experienced and inexperienced students. However, creating these extra modules may require extra work for instructors/TAs. A way to ensure this would be helpful to try is to gather intel from the students themselves about whether beginner modules sound like a good idea.
